activation_goal.label: freeze real upstream Hermes-Agent connection proof and service-safe cutover preflight
activation_goal.summary: RedCube must stop treating repo-local Hermes naming as runtime ownership proof, freeze one real upstream Hermes-Agent connection contract, and fail closed until a reachable upstream API server plus provider-backed run surface are verifiably live.

fresh_evidence: REDCUBE_HERMES_GATEWAY_COMMAND='<known-good upstream gateway launch command>' scripts/verify.sh integration

cutover_scope.service_safe_entry_goal: OPL Gateway -> RedCube domain adapter -> upstream Hermes-Agent runtime

verified_preconditions: provider credentials configured inside upstream Hermes-Agent
non-loopback upstream hosts also expose REDCUBE_HERMES_UPSTREAM_API_KEY
known_external_risks: upstream Hermes currently crashes on `hermes gateway run` without `-q` because `gateway/run.py` references `RedactingFormatter` before definition; RedCube records this as an upstream blocker rather than repo-local proof
verification hosts whose global `hermes` CLI still points at the broken upstream checkout must use `REDCUBE_HERMES_GATEWAY_COMMAND` to launch a known-good upstream gateway command explicitly
verification hosts whose upstream Hermes virtualenv lacks Playwright must set or auto-resolve `REDCUBE_PYTHON_COMMAND` to a Playwright-enabled Python for screenshot_review and export helpers
excluded_scope: repo-local Hermes naming repackaged as upstream proof
chat UI or natural-language shell work
